name: stAirCase v1.0 — AI Workspace Orchestrator
description: Bash CLI that orchestrates product repos <-> AI context repos for ralph-tui and agentic workflows, with atomic JSON state, jq-based manifest management, optional symlinks, dual hooks system, and cross-platform portability.

userStories.title: Colored output, --dry-run, and environment flags
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all staircase commands to support --dry-run and produce consistent colored output so that I can understand and audit actions before they run.
acceptanceCriteria: Color helpers: _info (cyan →), _ok (green ✔), _warn (yellow ⚠), _err (red ✖) — all write to stderr
NO_COLOR=1 disables all ANSI codes (CI-safe)
STAIRCASE_DEBUG=1 enables xtrace-style command logging
--dry-run is parsed globally and respected by every command that writes state
--dry-run outputs a [DRY RUN] prefix on every action that would be taken
--version prints staircase 1.0.0
--help prints usage with all commands and flags
bats test: NO_COLOR suppresses ANSI, --version prints version, --help prints usage, unknown commands exit 1

userStories.title: Workspace init
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run `staircase init` in any directory so that the workspace scaffolding is created safely and idempotently.
acceptanceCriteria: Creates product/, agent/, .staircase/tmp/ directories
Creates .staircase/manifest.json with {"version":"1.0","projects":{}} if not present
Re-running staircase init is a no-op (no error, no data loss)
Outputs ✔ initialized or ✔ already initialized
--dry-run prints what would be created without touching disk
All output goes to stderr; stdout is clean
bats test: init on empty dir, idempotent re-init, manifest schema, stderr-only output

userStories.title: Register a project
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run `staircase register <app> [--symlink]` so that an existing product repo is wired to a new agent context directory.
acceptanceCriteria: Creates product/<app>/ and agent/<app>/active/, agent/<app>/tasks/, agent/<app>/structure/
Adds entry to .staircase/manifest.json via atomic write (mktemp + mv)
jq is used for all manifest reads and writes — no string-concat JSON
--symlink flag creates product/<app>/tasks → ../../agent/<app>/active and sets symlinkEnabled: true
Errors if <app> is already registered (exit 1)
Errors if run outside a workspace (exit 1)
bats test: register new app, duplicate (exit 1), missing name (exit 1), outside workspace (exit 1), --symlink creates link + manifest flag

userStories.title: Create a new task
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run `staircase task new <app> <task-id>` so that a task context is created and set as the active task.
acceptanceCriteria: Creates agent/<app>/tasks/<task-id>/ directory
Writes agent/<app>/active/context.json via jq -n (safe with special characters in task IDs)
context.json schema: { taskId, app, created (ISO-8601), stories: [], files: [], gitDiff: "" }
Updates manifest.json activeTask field atomically
Refreshes symlink if enabled for the app
Errors if <app> is not registered (exit 1)
bats test: create on registered app, schema validation, unregistered app (exit 1), special chars in task ID, symlink refresh

userStories.title: Switch active task
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run `staircase task switch <app> <task-id> [--symlink]` so that I can atomically rotate between existing tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: Saves current active/context.json to tasks/<previousTaskId>/context.json
Copies tasks/<task-id>/context.json → active/context.json atomically
Updates manifest.json activeTask to <task-id>
--symlink flag enables symlink mode one-shot (persisted in manifest)
Refreshes existing symlink if enabled
Errors if <task-id> directory does not exist (exit 1 + suggestion)
bats test: switch between tasks, context saved/restored, non-existent task (exit 1), --symlink one-shot, symlink refresh

userStories.title: List tasks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run `staircase task list <app>` so that I can see all tasks for an app with the active one marked.
acceptanceCriteria: Lists all task directories under agent/<app>/tasks/
Active task marked with *
Shows last-modified timestamp per task
Errors if <app> is not registered (exit 1)
bats test: all tasks shown, active marker, unregistered app (exit 1), empty tasks dir

userStories.title: Symlink management
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to manage symlinks between product and agent directories so that tools can read context directly from product repos.
acceptanceCriteria: staircase symlink enable <app> creates relative symlink product/<app>/tasks → ../../agent/<app>/active
staircase symlink disable <app> removes symlink and clears manifest flag
staircase symlink status shows table with enabled state, link health, and resolved target
Symlinks auto-refresh on task new and task switch for enabled apps
Idempotent — enable on already-enabled exits 0, disable on already-disabled exits 0
Fails if product/<app>/tasks is a real directory
doctor detects missing/broken symlinks; doctor --fix repairs them
bats test: enable/disable/status, idempotent, readable through symlink, real dir blocks, switch cycle survival

userStories.title: Run ralph-tui for a project
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run `staircase run <app>` so that ralph-tui launches in the correct product directory with the active context injected.
acceptanceCriteria: Changes into product/<app>/ before launching
Launches ralph-tui --prd ../agent/<app>/active/context.json as a child process (not exec)
--agent-config '<json>' merges config into context.json before launch
Runs hooks.d/99-ralph-tui.sh after ralph-tui exits
Errors if product dir or context.json missing (exit 1 with hints)
--dry-run prints resolved command without executing
bats test: missing product (exit 1), missing context (exit 1), dry-run, dry-run with --agent-config

userStories.title: Workspace inspection (ls + status)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `staircase ls` and `staircase status` to show workspace state at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: ls prints tree view: root, app names (bold/cyan), product paths, agent paths, task count, active task (green), symlink state
ls shows missing product paths in red without crashing
status prints table: APP, PRODUCT PATH, ACTIVE TASK, LAST MODIFIED
status --json outputs full manifest as pretty-printed JSON
Both exit 0 with zero registered projects
bats test: ls tree/apps/tasks/symlink, status table/json, no workspace handling

userStories.title: Doctor command (self-healing)
userStories.description: As a developer or CI system, I want `staircase doctor [--fix]` to detect and repair broken workspace state.
acceptanceCriteria: Check 1: manifest.json exists and is valid JSON (fix: recreate empty manifest)
Check 2: registered apps have product/ and agent/ paths (fix: deregister)
Check 3: apps with activeTask have active/context.json (fix: create empty)
Check 4: .staircase/tmp/ exists and is writable (fix: create it)
Check 5: orphaned agent/ directories not in manifest (report)
Check 6: apps with symlinkEnabled have valid symlinks (fix: recreate)
--fix applies all repairs; without it, only reports
Exits 0 if healthy; exits 1 if issues remain
bats test: healthy, missing tmp, invalid manifest, missing paths, orphans, broken symlinks, multi-issue repair

userStories.title: Export command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `staircase export <app> [--format json|tar]` to back up or migrate agent context.
acceptanceCriteria: Default --format json: writes manifest + active context + all saved tasks to stdout
--format tar: creates staircase-export-<app>-<date>.tar.gz
JSON includes app, exported_at, manifest, agent.active, agent.tasks
Errors if <app> is not registered (exit 1)
--dry-run prints what would be exported without writing
bats test: json valid, active context, saved tasks, tar creates file, unknown app (exit 1), dry-run

userStories.title: Git hooks install
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `staircase hooks install <app>` to wire up Git hooks and lifecycle hooks.
acceptanceCriteria: Creates hooks.d/ with stubs: 01-format.sh, 99-ralph-tui.sh
Installs pre-commit hook running hooks.d/01-format.sh
Installs post-merge hook running staircase doctor --fix silently
Stubs and git hooks are executable
Existing hooks preserved — stAirCase guard block appended
Idempotent — guard block appears exactly once after multiple installs
Requires product/<app>/.git to exist
bats test: creates hooks, executable, idempotent guard, preserves existing, no git repo (exit 1)

userStories.title: Explain command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `staircase explain <command> [args]` to preview what a command would do as a structured diff.
acceptanceCriteria: Shows filesystem changes (created dirs, modified files)
Shows manifest JSON diff (before → after)
Shows context.json preview for task commands
Does not modify workspace state
Supports: init, register, task new, task switch
Exits 1 for unsupported commands
bats test: explain init/register/task new/task switch, no workspace mutation, missing args (exit 1)

userStories.title: Repository structure and CI
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want the repository to have tests, CI, and a demo workspace so that the project is shippable.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/integration.bats — 122 tests covering all commands (happy + error paths)
.github/workflows/ci.yml runs shellcheck and bats on push/PR
examples/demo-workspace/README.md — interactive walkthrough covering all features
README.md — developer-friendly documentation
CHANGELOG.md — single 1.0.0 release
LICENSE — MIT license (Copyright Botond Biro)
--version prints staircase 1.0.0
